title: Implement Stack using Arrays

content.problemStatement:
Here's a simple exercise that involves implementing a stack. 

In this exercise, you'll implement a basic stack to reverse a string using the stack's LIFO property.

TaskUpdate the functions `push()` and `pop()` within the class stack to output the reverses string.
constraints: S consists of printable ASCII characters (including spaces)
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S (may include spaces).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the characters of S in reverse order on a separate line, preserving all characters exactly as they appear.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each string is reversed using the stack, producing the reversed characters.
The first string is reversed, the second string is a palindrome and remains unchanged.
Spaces at the beginning and end are preserved and also reversed.

solution.approach: We simulate a stack using a character array. For each input string we push every character onto the array (incrementing a top index) and then pop them by reading the array backwards. This naturally yields the reversed string because a stack follows LIFO order. The algorithm runs in linear time per test case and uses O(|S|) auxiliary space for the stack.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case, where L is the length of the string. Overall O(total length of all strings).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) auxiliary space per test case for the character array used as a stack.
